name: Homefront Tax Loans
description_short: Hunter-Kelsey provides property tax loans in Texas, allowing homeowners and business owners to pay delinquent property taxes upfront and repay over time, avoiding county penalties and fees.
description_long:
Hunter-Kelsey of Texas is a property tax lending company based in Austin that specializes in helping Texas residents and businesses address delinquent property tax obligations. The company has originated over 30,000 property tax loans since its founding, positioning itself as an established lender in this niche market. They are regulated by the State of Texas and maintain membership in the Texas Property Tax Lienholders Association, indicating compliance with industry standards.

The company offers secured loans specifically designed to pay off residential and commercial property taxes across Texas. Their core service involves collecting basic borrower information, structuring a repayment plan, and directly paying the delinquent taxes to the county. This eliminates the accumulating penalties and interest that counties charge on delinquent accounts—which can reach 47% in combined penalties, interest, and attorney fees within the first year according to their marketing materials. They emphasize speed and simplicity, claiming the process can typically be completed over the phone.

Hunter-Kelsey distinguishes itself through specialization in a specific debt problem rather than general personal lending. They focus exclusively on property tax debt in Texas, allowing them to develop expertise in county-specific procedures and penalty structures. Their marketing emphasizes the speed of penalties (framing them as increasing 2% by end of April, equivalent to 24% annual rates) to create urgency around their solution. They provide both residential and commercial property tax loan options, serving a broader client base than residential-only lenders.

A key limitation is geographic scope—the company operates only in Texas. Additionally, while they present themselves as a solution to avoid penalties and fees, they are themselves a lender charging interest on the loan principal. The website does not disclose specific interest rates, terms, or APR information, making it impossible to verify whether their loans actually save borrowers money compared to other financing options. The absence of transparent pricing information is a significant consumer protection concern. Borrowers should verify whether the interest charged on the tax loan exceeds the county penalties they would otherwise pay.
pros: 30+ years of experience originating over 30,000 property tax loans in Texas
Licensed and regulated by the State of Texas with membership in Texas Property Tax Lienholders Association
Fast turnaround claiming process can be completed over the phone
Serves both residential and commercial property owners across all Texas counties
Directly pays taxes to county, eliminating need for borrower to navigate county payment processes
4.7 Google rating based on 214 customer reviews
Prevents accumulation of county penalties, interest, and attorney collection fees
cons: No APR, interest rates, or loan terms disclosed on website—borrowers cannot compare actual costs before contact
Operates only in Texas, unavailable to out-of-state property owners
Requires collateral (the property itself) as security for the loan, creating lien risk
Marketing uses high-pressure tactics and urgency framing (countdown timers, penalty escalation language) without transparent pricing
No independent third-party reviews or regulatory complaint data provided; Google reviews alone insufficient to assess company practices
best_for: Texas homeowners with delinquent property taxes seeking to avoid escalating county penalties and potential foreclosure
Commercial property owners in Texas facing property tax debt with time pressure from lenders or county collection
Borrowers unable to pay property taxes in full who need structured repayment over time
diagnosis: Hunter-Kelsey is best for Texas property owners facing delinquent property taxes who need immediate payment to avoid compounding county penalties. However, borrowers must obtain transparent pricing information (APR, interest rate, term length) before applying to verify that the loan cost actually saves money compared to paying county penalties directly.
services: Delinquent property tax payment to county
Statewide Texas property tax lending across all counties
meta_description: Hunter-Kelsey offers property tax loans in Texas, helping owners avoid penalties by repaying delinquent taxes over time.
